title: Finite Conjugate Gradient Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite conjugate-gradient bad step-size QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact CG replay computes alpha0 = 1/4 from r0^T r0 / p0^T A p0 while a malformed row claims alpha0 = 1/3, isolating a compact equality conflict for the Farkas route.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional Krylov or conjugate-gradient rows only when they add distinct residual orthogonality, A-conjugacy, preconditioning, finite termination, or numerical-honesty pressure; keep general convergence, SPD hypotheses, roundoff, and preconditioner theory in Lean or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed SPD rational matrix, right-hand side, initial point, residuals, search directions, step sizes, beta update, A-conjugacy dot product, and exact two-step solution as deterministic exact-rational replay obligations.
Route the false first step-size row through a source-linked QF_LRA artifact and checked Farkas evidence after exact replay computes alpha0 = 1/4.
Expose the pack through the matrix computation, optimization, residual-bound, and exact-vs-floating learner and consumer query surfaces.

Keep general conjugate-gradient convergence, finite-termination theorems, SPD/preconditioner hypotheses, Krylov subspace theory, roundoff, and floating-point solver stability marked Lean or numerical-honesty horizon.
